archetype: Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) Fiber-Optic Seismic Event Classifier for Perimeter Intrusion Detection
description: This system converts kilometers of buried fiber-optic cable into a continuous array of thousands of virtual microphones to detect, classify, and localize intrusions (digging, cutting, foot traffic) in real-time for high-security facilities. It is used by defense contractors and utility operators to distinguish between genuine threats and environmental noise like wind or wildlife. The technical difficulty lies in processing terabits of phase-sensitive Rayleigh backscatter data with microsecond latency to differentiate subsurface vibration signatures without false positives.
rules: MUST adhere to IEC 62443-3-3 for Industrial Security while processing real-time sensor streams from legacy interrogator hardware.
MUST implement adaptive signal processing algorithms to filter seismic noise caused by nearby highway traffic or wind, using the fiber's spatial multiplexing capabilities.
MUST comply with NERC CIP standards for real-time event logging and audit trails in Critical Bulk Electric System perimeters.
MUST handle phase unwrapping and coherent noise cancellation across 10km+ fiber spans where signal attenuation exceeds 25dB.
MUST utilize deterministic scheduling to guarantee sub-100ms end-to-end latency from optical interrogation to alarm trigger for automated gate closure.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard batch processing or ETL pipelines, as the physical security threat requires immediate reaction to vibration signatures before an intruder breaches the perimeter.
DO NOT rely on generic cloud-native auto-scaling for the initial signal acquisition layer, as network jitter from public internet links will corrupt the phase-sensitive interferometry data.

DO NOT implement static threshold-based alarm triggers, as environmental conditions (temperature, soil moisture) dynamically alter the fiber's acoustic coupling, requiring adaptive machine learning models.
key_technical_challenges: Separating coherent Rayleigh noise from actual intruder signatures in a non-stationary environmental noise floor.
Synchronizing distributed laser interrogators across multi-kilometer spans with nanosecond precision to enable coherent triangulation.
Optimizing deep learning inference for ultra-high-dimensional time-series data on edge devices with limited thermal and power budgets.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety for concurrent high-frequency data processing without garbage collection pauses, critical for deterministic real-time performance.
recommended_stack.framework: Tokio — Asynchronous runtime optimized for handling thousands of concurrent IO-bound optical sensor streams with minimal latency.
recommended_stack.database_engine: ClickHouse — Columnar database optimized for analytical queries on massive time-series datasets generated by continuous acoustic sensing.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Cluster — For rapid state management of active threat zones and real-time alarm deduplication.
recommended_stack.message_broker: NATS JetStream — High-throughput messaging system capable of guaranteeing delivery of critical intrusion alerts with strict ordering.
